content.claim: A quasi-experimental study of 600 students displaced from rural Finnish schools closed for cost reasons (not performance failure) in 1999–2000 found measurable short-term achievement disruption from consolidation itself, isolating the 'pure consolidation shock' independent of prior school quality.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Short-term outcome focus (length of disruption period not specified); long-run re-convergence not addressed
Sample size (600 students) and regional scope not fully specified; generalizability to current demographic context unclear
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
